answer.recommended_action: Measure laser diode forward voltage and driver-board output current to partition the −11.2% transmit-power loss (ev.laser_power_check_01) between intrinsic emitter-junction degradation and laser driver decay (hyp.upstream_power). If drive current is at spec, the emitter itself is degraded → replace laser emitter assembly. If drive current is depressed, the driver circuit is the proximate cause → repair or replace the driver board. TEC/thermal path is confirmed nominal (ev.tec_load_check_01) and need not be re-examined. The system reboot is a non-causal coincident event (ev.trigger_demoted) and should not influence the repair path.

hypotheses.label: Window contamination attenuating outgoing/return beam, reducing effective range
Laser emitter output power declining due to aging or drive degradation, reducing SNR at range
Receiver/detector sensitivity drift (dark-current increase or gain shift) raising effective noise floor
TEC module degradation causing elevated or unstable operating temperature, affecting laser or detector performance
Signal-processing or calibration parameter drift (firmware/config change or coefficient decay) producing systematic range underestimation or sensitivity loss

evidence.summary: Laser output power measured at 42.5 mW vs baseline 47.9 mW (−11.2%), a sustained source-side power decline. Magnitude is physically consistent with the observed ~8% range degradation (range scales sub-linearly with power in SNR-limited regimes). This is a real, non-zero-variance, source-side measurement confirming reduced transmit power.

TEC current 1.21 A at 48% of the 2.5 A limit; diode temperature 25.29 °C is comfortably below the 26.0 °C setpoint maximum; losing_setpoint=false. Thermal control is fully nominal — the TEC is neither saturated nor struggling, and the laser junction is being held at the correct operating point. This eliminates a thermal/TEC fault as a contributor to the −11.2% transmit-power decline recorded in ev.laser_power_check_01, and removes the thermal-root explanation for hyp.laser_aging, making intrinsic laser or drive degradation the residual cause.
log.reboot: degradation onset predates it → coincident, not causal

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

With all six hypotheses at equal prior (log_odds 0.00) and zero evidence collected, the highest-information first measurement is transmitted optical power. Range scales as the fourth root of SNR in the link budget, so an ~8% range loss requires roughly a 37% power reduction — detectable cleanly by this check. A NOMINAL result argues strongly AGAINST hyp.laser_aging and against the laser-side contribution of hyp.upstream_power, shifting probability mass entirely toward optical-path loss (hyp.window_contamination), receiver-side issues (hyp.detector_drift, hyp.thermal_tec_fault), or signal-processing changes (hyp.calibration_drift). A DEGRADED result raises hyp.laser_aging and hyp.upstream_power while lowering the remainder. No prior check result exists, so this is not re-confirming anything — it is the first discriminator that cleaves the hypothesis set into two disjoint groups.

ev.laser_power_check_01 confirms a real 11.2% source-side power drop, so the open question is no longer whether power is down but WHY. Two surviving hypotheses share nearly equal log_odds: hyp.laser_aging (2.00) attributes the decline to intrinsic emitter/drive degradation, while hyp.thermal_tec_fault (1.50) proposes that a failing TEC has allowed operating temperature to rise, thermally derating the laser. tec_load_check directly probes TEC duty-cycle, set-point error, and thermal margin. A nominal result (TEC meeting set-point, low duty-cycle, stable temperature) argues strongly AGAINST thermal_tec_fault — decoupling it from the already-confirmed power loss — and consolidates hyp.laser_aging as root cause. An abnormal result (elevated duty-cycle, set-point overshoot, or rising trend) raises hyp.thermal_tec_fault and provides a causal chain: TEC degradation → thermal derating → reduced laser output → 8% range loss. Neither outcome re-confirms an already-settled hypothesis; each moves hyp.laser_aging and hyp.thermal_tec_fault in opposite directions, which is exactly the discriminating property required.
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential
